observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to verify their records relative to the status of the foregoing outstanding checks and make the necessary adjustment in the books in consideration with the following journal entry, hence to present the correct balance of the CIB-LCCA and GE accounts in FS.

Debit	Cash in Bank		P85,839.66
Credit		Prior Period Adjustment		P85,839.66
# To record reversion of stale checks issued in 2023
and previous years to the CIB

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to abide with the provisions of Section 114 and 121 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs: (a) record all deliveries using the Perpetual Inventory Method;  (b) coursed thru the inventory account all purchases of goods; and (c) record issuances as they take place. Hence, to present the correct amount of inventory and expense accounts in the FS.

Debit 	Inventory account		P345,955.00
Credit		Prior period Adjustment (PPA)	P345,955.00
# To correct the balance of inventory account which was
previously recorded as expense but were not yet distributed.
Note: The affected expense accounts shall be restated in the 2025 FS

For the proper management and monitoring of the receivable accounts, the Audit Team recommended that:
The LCE instruct the Accounting Office and the Implementing Office/s to intensify efforts on the collection of the long outstanding receivables and seek legal remedies, if necessary;
The LCE file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivables in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 16, 2016, especially those recognized in the books as impaired, if not possible, instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the specific receivables in the Notes to FS; and
The Municipal Accountant monitor and analyze on a regular basis, all receivable accounts to ensure collection when they become due and demandable.

The Audit Team likewise recommended that Management adapt the provisions of COA Circular 2023-008 for the cleansing or derecognition of dormant receivables from the books of accounts, hence, to present fairly the receivable account in the FS.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE:
instruct the Inventory Committee to prioritize the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory and reconcile the results thereon with the accounting records to ascertain the accuracy, completeness and existence of the reported PPE in the financial statement;
instruct the Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee to reclassify the properties costing below P50,000.00 to semi-expendable properties; and
instruct the Property Officer, the Head of Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to avail of the advantages of the COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 by adhering with its guidelines and procedures hence,  conduct of physical count of properties; recognition of PPE items found at station; disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items; and for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, in order to reflect more reliable and accurate balances of PPE accounts, thus to convey precise information useful in decision making and accountability for the properties.

The Audit Team recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the variance of P1,180,059.52 with the records of the Provincial Accountant to ensure correct and accurate balance of the Due to LGUs account in the FS and to ensure the correctness of the remittances made.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the SDO to limit her cash advance based on her maximum cash accountability or if the same is indispensable, require the Municipal Treasurer to cash advance for the excess amount hence not to exceed her cash accountability and direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary documents for the application of bond for the GSO and pay for the corresponding bond premiums, hence to protect the interest of the government.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the GSO to assign property/inventory number on properties and inventories that were acquired in 2024 and to continually ensure the proper tagging of property number to succeeding procurements of equipment and inventories to facilitate identification of properties which is useful in the conduct of annual physical inventory and to ascertain security and proper safeguarding of properties of the Municipality.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure the proper withholding of EWT from succeeding payments in accordance with Section 2(J) of RR No. 11-2018 hence, to avoid possible penalties and to help the government foster its tax collection.

The Audit Team recommended that the LCE instruct the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to conduct inventory of all unserviceable properties, after which, prepare the IIRUP and submit a copy to COA for its inspection and for the immediate disposition thereof, thus provide the Management additional income from the proceeds of the saleable unserviceable properties and from the use of the space where these were stored, also, the Property Custodian/Municipal Treasurer will be relieved from unnecessary accountability.

We recommended that the LCE:
instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to ensure the optimal utilization of the RA 7171 and 8240 Municipal Share through proper and timely implementation of the identified PPAs so that the benefits expected from its timely completion will be delivered to the tobacco farmers.
require the concerned officials to review the feasibility of the unimplemented projects and plan for the necessary realignment of these funds to a more viable development projects that help in the attainment of the purpose and objective of the fund which is to advance the self-reliance of the farmer beneficiaries.
propose to the Sanggunian the re-appropriation of the prior year’s savings from implemented projects thus, maximize implementation and utilization of the funds for the benefit of the tobacco farmers.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to update and balance daily her cashbooks/records to ensure the availability of sufficient funds to cover all issued checks, hence to avoid the incurrence of overdraft and payment of bank charges due to insufficient fund.

Likewise, to reconcile her records with the accounting records regularly to serve as an alternative measure to monitor the correct balances of the account and a control mechanism to prevent loss or misappropriation of funds.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the correct recording of the advance collection of real property tax due in the succeeding year and the recognition of income.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate or trace the documents of recorded infrastructure assets and make the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct book value of the foregoing infrastructure assets, hence to present fairly and reliably the FS.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer, being the Acting GSO, to accept all deliveries of procured goods before inspection and issuance thereof in adherence to Section 118 of the MNGAS to ensure proper accounting and recording of deliveries and monitoring of issuances thereof, thus to prevent loss of properties and misappropriation of funds.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the officials and employees concerned in the processing of payment for terminal leave benefits, more specifically the approving authorities, revisit the guidelines before allowing payment for any claims. In light of the foregoing observation, it is worth emphasizing that the payment of terminal leave is based on the highest monthly salary received at any time during the employee’s period of employment in the government service and not on his latest salary, unless the latter is the highest received by the employee.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal officials and employees responsible in the processing of payment for terminal pay already follow the guidelines provided in the payment of terminal pay.
observations.reason: Post-audit of the transactions of the Municipality pertaining to payment of terminal pay for the year ended December 31, 2017 revealed that the guidelines in the payment are already followed.

observations.recommendation: For proper observance of the circular, we have recommended that disbursement transactions including reimbursements be supported with principal evidence of receipt of payment such as Sales Invoice (Cash or Charge) or Official Receipts. If Charge Sales Invoice is attached, it should be supplemented with a Collection Receipt as a proof of receipt of the payment. Management should enjoin in the observance of the circular in all transactions of the Municipality to eliminate possible risks about the payment of disbursements, in addition, to help the Bureau of the Internal Revenue in the exercise of its functions.
observations.managementAction: Management already complies with RMC No. 2-2014 as shown by the decrease of instances of non-compliance noted in the audit.
observations.reason: One (1) transaction of non-compliance with RMC No. 2-2014 was noted in the post-audit of the disbursements of the Municipality for CY 2017. The transaction was suspended in the audit.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the officials and employees claiming and approving travel and transportation expenses follow the prescribed rules and regulations on local and foreign travels so that expenses claimed will be granted uniformly and accordingly. Also, we encourage the approving authorities to implement controls (e.g. require original copy of the CA, not just mere photocopies) to mitigate any possible fraudulent activities on the claiming of travel and transportation expenses.
observations.managementAction: There was a decrease of instances of non-compliance noted in the audit.
observations.reason: Two (2) instances of non-compliance with EO no. 298 and COA Circular No. 96-004 on the claims for travel and transportation expenses were noted in the post-audit of the disbursements of the Municipality for the CY 2017. These transactions were suspended in the audit.

observations.recommendation:
The requisitioning department and/or end-user units along with the BAC see to it that specifications for the procurement of goods be based on relevant characteristics, functionality and/or performance. A generic description of the product must be used instead of referring to brands and that reference to brand names should only be allowed for items or parts that are compatible with the existing fleet or equipment of the same make or brand to maintain the performance, functionality and useful life of the equipment.
The BAC invite observers in all stages of the procurement process (i.e. eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, preliminary examination of bids, bid evaluation, and post-qualification) in accordance with Section 13 of the revised IRR of RA 9184 to enhance transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The BAC implemented the invitation of representative from COA and at least two (2) observers during the Bid Evaluation stage of the procurement process. However, for the reference to brand names, the BAC has no action taken for CY 2017.
observations.reason: Post-audit showed that all of the procurement transactions (through public bidding) of the Municipality for the CY 2017 have attached invitations to COA and at least (two) 2 observers for the Bid Evaluation Stage. However, verification of the purchase requests of 17 procurement transactions of the Municipality for CY 2017 revealed that they referred to brand names.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the submission of Accomplishment Report which the Municipal Engineer promised to comply.
observations.reason: Post-audit of the project Dredging of Sinait Rivers and Creeks for CY 2017 showed that the Accomplishment Report for the work done is already attached in the disbursement vouchers which indicates implementation of the audit observation.

observations.reason: Review and analysis on the implementation of the Municipality of its PPAs under the 20% Development Fund for CY 2017 revealed that almost all the projects were fully implemented or partially implemented.

A report thereon should be prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Furthermore, an Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Properties should be prepared to account for municipal properties which are already beyond economical repair to facilitate the dropping of these from the books to ensure fairness of financial statement presentation.
observations.managementAction: The inventory committee which management created to conduct the physical inventory count ofMunicipal PPE had not completed the count due to lack of time.
observations.reason: There was no annual inventory report for the calendar year 2016 received by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the management agreed to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 such that POs and all supporting documents thereto are submitted to the Audit Team within the period prescribed to facilitate timely review of the same.
observations.managementAction: The management is trying to get by on the submission of the PO’s within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Records of the Audit Team on the receipt ofthe copies ofPOs revealed that there were still delays on the submission of some of the POs of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the employees to transfer the registration of these parcels of land in the name ofthe municipality and cause the titling of the same.
observations.managementAction: The municipality cannot act accordingly because there were some provisions of the law which the parties concerned were not able to comply with.
observations.reason: Verification revealed that the said parcels of land were not yet transferred in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare an accurate list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the list as his basis to record the RPT and SET Receivables in the books. This should have been accomplished at the beginning of each year in compliance with Section 20 of Volume I of the MNGAS. As regards to the unrecorded RPT and SET Receivables for the year 2015, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her data with the Municipal Accountant so that accurate balances of the RPT and SET Receivables will be reported in the financial statements to reflect the correct financial position of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The List of taxpayers with corresponding amount of taxes due was prepared but submitted late.Nonetheless, the Municipal Accountant was able to record in the books on January 2016 the requiredRPT and SET receivables for 2015.
observations.reason: Verification of the financial statements for 2016 revealed that the required RPT and SET receivables for 2015 was already recorded in the books, likewise with the RPT and SET receivables for 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the management agreed that regular purchases of drugs and medicines for stock be recorded as an inventory and shall only be recognized as an expense upon their issuance for proper presentation in the financial statements and for proper monitoring of the consumption thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant overlooked the provisions under PPSAS No. 12. Moving forward, the medicines for RHU operations and Medicines for LDRRM operations had already been taken up as inventories for CY 2016.
observations.reason: Post-audit of transactions of the Municipality forthe CY 2016 revealed that these purchases had been properly taken up in the books.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that there should be Acknowledgement Receipts from the recipients specifying the supplies and materials received including their corresponding  amounts. A copy thereof should be furnished to the Accounting Office for  proper recording and accounting in the books of the barangays. Monitoring and inspection of the  projects should also be carried out to ensure proper implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: To resolve the issue pertaining to lack of documents supporting the transactions, the municipality planned to conduct public bidding for the repair & maintenance of Barangay Roads for the CY 2016.
observations.reason: Post-audit of the transactions of the Municipality for CY 2016 revealed that the transactions pertaining to the observation have been properly supported the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the LGU adhere to the provision on the correct computation of the Subsistence and Laundry Allowances. Likewise, we encourage that Daily Time Records (DTR) should be properly filled-up so that official travels will be properly indicated in the PHWs’ DTRs for easier reference. In addition, the PHWs should refund the excess allowances that they received.
observations.managementAction: Management already adhered to the provisions on the correct computation of the Subsistence and Laundry Allowances beginning April, 2016. Likewise DTR’s were already properly accomplished before payment of the allowances.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payments of Subsistence and Laundry Allowances for the calendar year 2016 showed that the Municipality already followed to the correct computation of the SLA.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipal offices concerned employ the necessary procedures to facilitate the collection of the said receivables. They should intensify the collection procedures by sending notice of collection to the borrowers to remind them of their obligations or coordinating with the barangay officials in the collection ofloans granted to their respective constituents. Moreover, guidelines should be provided to ensure that the livelihood projects are feasible and viable and that the beneficiaries are capable to maintain and sustain their respective identified livelihood projects. For the dormant accounts of which the corresponding loan borrowers’ whereabouts could no longer be located, management should request for write-off for fair presentation of the receivable account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management served notices of collection to the delinquent borrowers. Likewise, they have been planning to sort to other collection strategies.
observations.reason: Review of the financial statement accounts of the Municipality for CY 2016 showed that there was a decrease of the Loans Receivable account amounting to P300,352.12 as compared with the prior year’s balance.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipality implement fully the projects programmed under the 20% Development Fund in order to fully attain the purpose/objectives forwhich the fund was created, which is for the economic and social development of the beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction:
Among the projects in 2015 only 5 projects had not been implemented:

Fingerlings Dispersal (P5,000.00);
Soil management/Organic Fertilizer Production (P50,000.00); c)Bottle Crusher (P290,000.00)
Improvement of Controlled Dumpsite (P360,000.00);and 
Purchase of Hybrid Corn Seeds-100,000.00.
The purchase of hybrid corn seeds was not implemented because therewas a same project of the Provincial Government.
observations.reason: Review and analysis conducted on the expenditures of the Municipality for CY 2016 revealed that the PPAs under the 20% Development Fund were not fully implemented for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that disallowances be settled/appealed within six (6) months from receipt of the notice.
observations.managementAction: The audit disallowances are under appeal.
observations.reason: There was no settlement made by the Municipality as of the year ended December 31, 2016.
